perturbation.original_text: 1.5 Quality Control, Licensor Approvals. Licensor, as owner of the Licensed Mark, shall have the right at all times to control and approve the nature and quality of the Licensed Products (and the Licensed Mark thereon), and to inspect Licensee's business operations upon reasonable prior notice for the purpose of ensuring that a high level of quality of the Licensed Products is being maintained by Licensee. At Licensor's reasonable request during each calendar year, Licensee shall submit samples to Licensor, at no cost to Licensor, and shall not materially depart therefrom without Licensor's prior express written consent. The Licensed Products, as well as all promotional, packaging and advertising material relative thereto, shall include all appropriate legal notices as required by Licensor. No more frequently than once per year, a third party auditor chosen by Licensor and approved by Licensee, such approval not to be unreasonably withheld, shall be entitled at any time on reasonable notice to the Licensee to enter, during regular business hours, any premises used by the Licensee or its manufacturers for the manufacture, packaging or storage of the Licensed Products, to inspect such premises, all plant, workforce and machinery used for manufacture, packaging or storage of Licensed Products and all other aspects of the manufacture, packaging and storage of Licensed Products ("Access Rights"). Prior to exercising such Access Rights, the third party auditor shall enter into a nondisclosure agreement with Licensee that, among other terms deemed acceptable by Licensee and such third party auditor, shall: (a) limit the content of any report made by the third party auditor to Licensor to a description of the manner in which, and the conditions under which, the Licensed Mark are used by Licensee or its manufacturers; and (b) prevent the disclosure of any of Licensee's trade secrets and/or Confidential Information. To the extent reasonably practicable, all Licensed Products shall include notices on labeling and packaging for the Licensed Products stating that the Licensed Mark is owned by Licensor and used by Licensee under license from Licensor. The Licensed Products shall be of a quality commensurate with previous production or the samples approved by Licensor. If the quality of a class of the Licensed Products falls below such standards, Licensee shall use commercially reasonable efforts to restore such quality. In the event that Licensee has not taken appropriate steps to restore such quality within one-hundred twenty (120) days after notification by Licensor, Licensor shall have the right to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 Quality Control, Licensor Approvals. Licensor, as owner of the Licensed Mark, shall have the right to control the nature and quality of the Licensed Products (and the Licensed Mark thereon). At Licensor's reasonable request during each calendar year, Licensee shall submit samples to Licensor, at no cost to Licensor, and shall not materially depart therefrom without Licensor's prior express written consent. The Licensed Products, as well as all promotional, packaging and advertising material relative thereto, shall include all appropriate legal notices as required by Licensor. The Licensed Products shall be of a quality commensurate with previous production or the samples approved by Licensor. If the quality of a class of the Licensed Products falls below such standards, Licensee shall use commercially reasonable efforts to restore such quality. In the event that Licensee has not taken appropriate steps to restore such quality within one-hundred twenty (120) days after notification by Licensor, Licensor shall have the right to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the text 'and to inspect Licensee's business operations upon reasonable prior notice for the purpose of ensuring that a high level of quality of the Licensed Products is being maintained by Licensee. No more frequently than once per year, a third party auditor chosen by Licensor and approved by Licensee, such approval not to be unreasonably withheld, shall be entitled at any time on reasonable notice to the Licensee to enter, during regular business hours, any premises used by the Licensee or its manufacturers for the manufacture, packaging or storage of the Licensed Products, to inspect such premises, all plant, workforce and machinery used for manufacture, packaging or storage of Licensed Products and all other aspects of the manufacture, packaging and storage of Licensed Products ("Access Rights"). Prior to exercising such Access Rights, the third party auditor shall enter into a nondisclosure agreement with Licensee that, among other terms deemed acceptable by Licensee and such third party auditor, shall: (a) limit the content of any report made by the third party auditor to Licensor to a description of the manner in which, and the conditions under which, the Licensed Mark are used by Licensee or its manufacturers; and (b) prevent the disclosure of any of Licensee's trade secrets and/or Confidential Information. To the extent reasonably practicable, all Licensed Products shall include notices on labeling and packaging for the Licensed Products stating that the Licensed Mark is owned by Licensor and used by Licensee under license from Licensor.', the quality control section lacks the ability for Licensor to inspect Licensee's business operation. However, the Licensor stills has the right to control the nature and quality of the Licensed Products. The omission introduces an inconsistency regarding how the 'right to control' will be enforced without on-site inspections or third-party audits, potentially leading to disputes over quality standards and compliance. It contradicts within the same section the control over quality without a mean to enforce such control. The main purpose of the section its to allow for quality control and by ommitting the middle part the original intent is being affected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.5 Quality Control, Licensor Approvals. Licensor, as owner of the Licensed Mark, shall have the right to control the nature and quality of the Licensed Products (and the Licensed Mark thereon). At Licensor's reasonable request during each calendar year, Licensee shall submit samples to Licensor, at no cost to Licensor, and shall not materially depart therefrom without Licensor's prior express written consent. The Licensed Products, as well as all promotional, packaging and advertising material relative thereto, shall include all appropriate legal notices as required by Licensor. The Licensed Products shall be of a quality commensurate with previous production or the samples approved by Licensor. If the quality of a class of the Licensed Products falls below such standards, Licensee shall use commercially reasonable efforts to restore such quality. In the event that Licensee has not taken appropriate steps to restore such quality within one-hundred twenty (120) days after notification by Licensor, Licensor shall have the right to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text omits the Licensor's right to inspect the Licensee's business operations, which directly contradicts the statement in the same section granting the Licensor control over the quality of the Licensed Products. The omission creates an inconsistency because the Licensor's ability to effectively control quality is significantly impaired without the ability to conduct inspections or audits, directly impacting the intent of the quality control section as a whole. The original text was: 'Licensor, as owner of the Licensed Mark, shall have the right at all times to control and approve the nature and quality of the Licensed Products (and the Licensed Mark thereon), and to inspect Licensee's business operations upon reasonable prior notice for the purpose of ensuring that a high level of quality of the Licensed Products is being maintained by Licensee.' and the changed text is: 'Licensor, as owner of the Licensed Mark, shall have the right to control the nature and quality of the Licensed Products (and the Licensed Mark thereon).' Thus, there is no inspection permitted by the Licensor, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.4 Survival of Obligations; Return of Confidential Information. Notwithstanding any expiration or termination of this Agreement, Sections 1.4, 3, 4.4, 5.1, 5.2, and 6.1 through 6.11 shall survive and continue to be enforceable as set forth herein. Upon any expiration or termination of this Agreement, Licensee shall promptly return to Licensor, or at Licensor's direction, destroy all Licensor confidential information and all copies thereof in Licensee's possession.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.4 Survival of Obligations; Return of Confidential Information. Notwithstanding any expiration or termination of this Agreement, Sections 1.4, 5.1, 5.2, and 6.1 through 6.11 shall survive and continue to be enforceable as set forth herein. Upon any expiration or termination of this Agreement, Licensee shall promptly return to Licensor, or at Licensor's direction, destroy all Licensor confidential information and all copies thereof in Licensee's possession.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the survival of section 3 from the surviving obligations, the indemnification clause (Section 3) does not survive the termination of the contract. This is an internal contradiction because contracts typically require certain protective clauses like indemnification to remain in effect even after the contract ends, especially concerning liabilities that may arise from activities during the contract term. This omission can affect the original intent by leaving Licensor unprotected. The contract no longer protects Licensor from post-termination liabilities related to the Licensed Products or Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark. This affects the risk allocation initially intended by the parties. This create uncertainty on how to manage Liabilities after termination or expiration of the contract. Section 4.4 now contradicts the implicit understanding of the contract's risk management framework.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. INDEMNIFICATION. Licensee shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless Licensor and its officers, directors, employees, agents, corporate subsidiaries, parents, and affiliates ("Licensor Indemnitees") from and against any and all demands, claims, actions or causes of action, assessments, deficiencies, damages, losses, liabilities and expenses (including, without limitation, reasonable expenses of investigation and attorneys' fees and expenses), incurred in conjunction with or arising out of or relating to any third-party claim concerning the Licensed Products and any acts or omissions of Licensee with respect to the Licensed Mark, including without limitation Licensee's performance of its obligations under this Agreement. The Licensor Indemnitees agree to cooperate with Licensee, at Licensee's expense, to provide copies of any documents or materials reasonably requested by Licensee in support of its defense of the Licensor Indemnitees.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that Section 3 (Indemnification) survives termination. The changed text omits Section 3 from the list of surviving obligations. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (Section 3), as the indemnification clause would no longer be enforceable after the contract terminates, leaving Licensor unprotected from liabilities as initially intended.

perturbation.original_text: 6.10 Assignment. This Agreement may not be assigned by Licensee without the consent of Licensor which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no such consent of Licensor is required under this Agreement in the event of a Change of Control of Licensee so long as: (a) the resulting, surviving or transferee Person assumes all the obligations of the Licensee by operation of Law or pursuant to an agreement in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Licensor; and (b) the licenses granted herein shall not be transferrable or sublicensable to Affiliates of such Person unless such Affiliates were Affiliates of Licensee prior to such Change of Control.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.10 Assignment. This Agreement may not be assigned by Licensee without the consent of Licensor which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no such consent of Licensor is required under this Agreement in the event of a Change of Control of Licensee so long as: (a) the resulting, surviving or transferee Person assumes all the obligations of the Licensee by operation of Law or pursuant to an agreement in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Licensor.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting condition (b) from section 6.10 (Assignment), the license granted herein can now be transferable or sublicensable to Affiliates of the Person resulting from the Change of Control of Licensee, even if they weren't Affiliates of Licensee prior to such Change of Control. This directly contradicts the original intent of the assignment clause, which was to limit the transferability of the license to only those affiliates that were already affiliated with the Licensee before a change of control. The original clause aims to restrict the scope of the license post-acquisition, preventing the acquirer from extending the licensed rights to new affiliates. Without this restriction, the value of the licensed mark could be diluted if the acquirer extends its use to a broader network of previously unaffiliated entities. It opens the door for potential misuse or overextension of the licensed mark, diluting its exclusivity and potentially impacting Licensor's brand control. This creates uncertainty, as it's unclear if Licensor needs to approve or if Affiliates of the new person/entity resulting from the Change of Control can sublicense the License without express consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.10 Assignment. This Agreement may not be assigned by Licensee without the consent of Licensor which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no such consent of Licensor is required under this Agreement in the event of a Change of Control of Licensee so long as: (a) the resulting, surviving or transferee Person assumes all the obligations of the Licensee by operation of Law or pursuant to an agreement in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Licensor.
perturbation.justification: The omission of condition (b) from section 6.10 (Assignment) allows the license granted to be transferable or sublicensable to Affiliates of the Person resulting from the Change of Control of Licensee, even if they weren't Affiliates of Licensee prior to such Change of Control. This directly contradicts the original intent as defined in the original text of the section 6.10, where (b) explicitly limits the transferability of the license. Because the changed text is the same location as the original text, and the original text now acts as the 'contradicted text', a contradiction is created due to the omission, as previously the license was restricted, and now is not.
